title: Ceradenia longipinnata
taxonomy: Ceradenia longipinnata belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. It is a species of fern that is native to certain regions. This plant is classified within the kingdom Plantae and further categorized under the division Pteridophyta. It is also classified as a member of the order Polypodiales. At the genus level, Ceradenia longipinnata falls under the genus Ceradenia.

intro: Ceradenia longipinnata, also known as Long-leaved Ceradenia, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceradenia longipinnata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
